Hysterosalpingogram, and laparoscopy and dye insufflation will gain similar information and either can be used to assess tubal patency in all patients.

A single semen analysis with a volume of 1 mL, a concentration of 10 million/mL and 30 per cent reduced motility confirms oligozoospermia, and serum testosterone, gonadotrophins and prolactin analysis should be performed.

answer: A semen analysis should be performed prior to laparoscopy and dye to test for tubal patency.
